pattern: The prefix sum up to x equals the total minus the prefix sum up to x-1, so the balance point can be found by closed-form or a single scan over candidate values.
hints: Write down what each side equals in terms of triangular numbers; the left side grows while the right side shrinks as x increases.
The left side is the sum 1..x and the right side is the sum x..n; set them equal and you get a relation between x squared and n.
Either scan x from 1 to n comparing the two running sums, or solve x*x = n*(n+1)/2 and return x only if that is a perfect square within range.

edge_inputs.description: smallest input where the pivot is the number itself
a value with a valid pivot

stress.description: every n in range exercised by sweeping the whole valid domain
stress.note: n is tiny so direct scan in reference is instant
